title: /ai Front Door
summary: The visitor's question leads: persona strip, sensor-backed proof strip, derived writing — replacing the owner-diary hub with two dead Live badges.

strategy.decision: Frame the front door as the visitor's question ('Work this way') with three derived strips — personas (diagnose), proof (evidence), writing (freshness) — instead of the owner-centric four-card academy hub.
strategy.why: The pilot's job is *find my craft persona, follow its demonstrated path* within two minutes (JTBD, `research/personas/craft-practitioner.md`). The old hero is owner's-diary voice — Cut verdict in `research/current-state/old-corpus-inventory.md` §1. Persona-diagnosis-by-existing-craft is the underserved step no competitor fuses with evidence (`01-diagnose.md` D6). Implements P1/P2/P4 of `02-prescription.yml` and 03-design-brief.md §1.
strategy.shipped: Light-purple academy hub: 'Working with AI' framed as Nino's documentation; four pathway cards (Learn/Build/Reference/Ask) with a false '70+ terms' stat and two 'Live' badges — one 500s in use, one 404s.
strategy.gap: Owner's diary with broken proof → visitor's map with sensor-backed proof; four doors collapse to Learn (primary) + Work (evidence) + Blog (derived).
strategy.question: Does the persona strip beat a single 'Start here' path for first-visit comprehension? Validate with 2-3 cold viewers at deploy review.

currentState.summary: 13-page section, all noindex, zero inbound links; front door badges two dead projects Live and inflates the reference count.
currentState.annotation: Proposed integrates the site nav (dark site system, not the ai-theme microsite skin), leads with the persona strip, and every stat/badge is derived or sensor-backed — nothing hand-asserted.
